docs.title: Portfolio Risk Engine
docs.text: GPU-accelerated Monte Carlo engine for simulating multi-asset portfolio dynamics and estimating risk metrics.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Monte Carlo Simulation: Simulate thousands of portfolio scenarios
  • Risk Metrics: Calculate VaR (Value at Risk) and Expected Shortfall
  • GPU Acceleration: Leverage CUDA for high-performance computations
  • Multi-Asset Support: Handle portfolios with multiple correlated assets

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
from portfolio_risk_engine import PortfolioSimulator

# Create simulator
simulator = PortfolioSimulator()

# Run simulation
results = simulator.run()

# Get risk metrics
var_95 = results.value_at_risk(0.95)
es_95 = results.expected_shortfall(0.95)

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
# Clone and setup
git clone https://github.com/romain-blanchot/montecarlo-portfolio-risk-gpu.git
cd montecarlo-portfolio-risk-gpu

# Install with dev dependencies
pip install -e .[dev]

# Run tests
pytest tests/

# Run linting
ruff check .

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This page documents the public API of the Portfolio Risk Engine.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: The Portfolio Risk Engine follows a layered (hexagonal) architecture for clean separation of concerns.

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
src/portfolio_risk_engine/
├── domain/           # Core business logic
├── application/      # Use cases and orchestration
├── infrastructure/   # External adapters (GPU, I/O)
├── cli.py           # CLI entry point
└── __main__.py      # Module execution support

docs.title: Domain Layer
docs.text:
Core business logic and domain models:

  • Risk Metrics: VaR, Expected Shortfall calculations
  • Portfolio Entities: Asset, Portfolio, Position models
  • Simulation Parameters: Configuration for Monte Carlo runs

docs.title: Application Layer
docs.text:
Use cases and orchestration:

  • Simulation Workflows: Coordinate simulation execution
  • Result Aggregation: Combine and process simulation outputs

docs.title: Infrastructure Layer
docs.text:
External adapters and integrations:

  • GPU Compute: CUDA/CuPy acceleration
  • Data Sources: Market data ingestion
  • I/O: File and network operations

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text:
CLI → Application → Domain ← Infrastructure
         ↓            ↑
    Use Cases    GPU Compute
  1. CLI parses user input and invokes application use cases
  2. Application layer orchestrates domain operations
  3. Infrastructure provides GPU acceleration and data access
  4. Results flow back through the layers to the user
